featured.title: RAGnamok
featured.tagline: A privacy-first, fully local Retrieval-Augmented Generation system designed for scalable document intelligence.
featured.summary: Designed and developed a modular Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system capable of ingesting multimodal PDF documents, generating structured knowledge representations, and answering user queries using local language models.
featured.problem: Most RAG solutions rely on external cloud APIs, making them unsuitable for privacy-sensitive documents while also limiting customization of the retrieval pipeline.
featured.solution: Built a fully local, modular RAG pipeline using FastAPI, PostgreSQL with pgvector, LangChain, and Ollama. The system supports asynchronous document ingestion, multi-vector retrieval, configurable pipelines, and REST APIs for seamless integration.
featured.impact: Provides a production-oriented foundation for privacy-preserving document intelligence while remaining fully extensible for future AI capabilities.
engineering_decisions: Adopted PostgreSQL with pgvector to leverage relational storage alongside vector search in a single database.
Implemented asynchronous processing to decouple document ingestion from user interactions.
Used a modular architecture to allow independent experimentation with parsers, retrievers, embeddings, and language models.
Designed the retrieval pipeline around Multi-Vector Retrieval to improve semantic recall beyond conventional chunk-based approaches.
challenges: Designing a flexible pipeline that supports multiple document processing strategies.
Balancing retrieval quality with indexing performance.
Maintaining modularity without sacrificing system simplicity.
lessons_learned: Retrieval quality often has a greater impact than model size.
Well-designed system architecture accelerates experimentation significantly.
Local AI systems require careful engineering beyond model implementation.

metrics.users: Evaluated by gemma4:31b-cloud as LLM Judge

featured.title: BISINDO Gesture Recognition Generalization
featured.tagline: Improving the generalization of BISINDO gesture recognition through realistic 3D keypoint augmentation.
featured.summary: Designed and developed a machine learning pipeline to improve the cross-domain generalization of BISINDO alphabet recognition by introducing scenario-based 3D keypoint augmentation using MediaPipe hand landmarks.
featured.problem: Gesture recognition models trained on a single subject often achieve excellent evaluation scores on internal datasets but fail to generalize to new users, devices, and real-world environments due to overfitting.
featured.solution: Proposed a realistic 3D keypoint augmentation framework that simulates natural hand variations before training an MLP classifier. The pipeline includes landmark normalization, feature engineering, augmentation, model training, and cross-domain evaluation.
featured.impact: Improved cross-domain F1-Score from 67.20% to 89.55% (+22.35%) while maintaining high internal performance, demonstrating a substantial improvement in model generalization.
engineering_decisions: Used MediaPipe 3D hand landmarks instead of raw images to reduce computational complexity while preserving gesture semantics.
Designed realistic augmentation strategies based on natural hand movement rather than random perturbations.
Implemented wrist-centered normalization and scale normalization to improve robustness across users.
Evaluated the model using an external dataset to measure real-world generalization instead of relying solely on internal validation.
challenges: Designing augmentation strategies that introduce realistic variations without distorting gesture semantics.
Preventing overfitting caused by single-subject training data.
Maintaining stable training performance while increasing data diversity.
lessons_learned: Improving generalization is often more valuable than pursuing higher training accuracy.
Carefully designed synthetic data can outperform simply collecting more data.
External evaluation is essential for validating real-world machine learning performance.
future_improvements: Explore Graph Neural Networks for hand landmark representation.
Investigate Transformer-based sequence modeling for gesture recognition.
Extend the system to real-time sentence-level BISINDO recognition.

metrics.dataset: Single-subject training dataset + external cross-domain evaluation dataset

featured.title: S.A.F.E. Laboratory
featured.tagline: An AIoT-based laboratory access system integrating computer vision and IoT for automated PPE compliance verification.
featured.summary: Developed the AI component of a smart laboratory access system that verifies Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) compliance in real time before granting laboratory access through an RFID-enabled attendance workflow.
featured.problem: Laboratory safety inspections are often performed manually, making them time-consuming, inconsistent, and prone to human error before users enter restricted laboratory areas.
featured.solution: Designed and trained a YOLOv5 object detection model for PPE detection, integrated through a Flask API with an IoT-based attendance system using ESP32 and RFID to automate laboratory entry validation.
featured.impact: Achieved 93–97% PPE detection accuracy and was selected as a Top 10 Finalist among 7,590 participants in Samsung Innovation Campus Batch 5.
engineering_decisions: Collected and manually annotated a custom PPE dataset to improve detection performance under realistic laboratory conditions.
Separated AI inference from the web application through a Flask API for modular deployment.
Integrated the AI model with IoT hardware to enable automated access control instead of standalone object detection.
Prioritized real-time inference speed while maintaining reliable detection accuracy.
challenges: Collecting sufficient PPE images with diverse lighting conditions and viewing angles.
Balancing detection accuracy with inference latency for real-time operation.
Integrating AI inference with RFID-based hardware communication.
lessons_learned: Deploying AI systems requires significantly more engineering than model training alone.
Dataset quality often contributes more to performance than architectural complexity.
System integration becomes the primary challenge once the model reaches acceptable accuracy.
future_improvements: Deploy optimized models using TensorRT or ONNX Runtime.
Expand PPE categories for broader industrial safety applications.
Implement edge deployment for lower latency.

featured.title: Route Optimization using ILS-RVND
featured.tagline: A web-based route optimization system implementing the Iterated Local Search with Random Variable Neighborhood Descent (ILS-RVND) algorithm.
featured.summary: Developed a complete optimization application that solves routing problems by implementing the ILS-RVND metaheuristic from scratch, allowing users to generate efficient travel routes through an interactive web interface.
featured.problem: Finding near-optimal routes for multiple destinations becomes computationally expensive as the number of locations increases, making brute-force approaches impractical for real-world applications.
featured.solution: Implemented the ILS-RVND optimization algorithm entirely from scratch in Python and deployed it as an interactive Streamlit application capable of solving routing problems with customizable datasets.
featured.impact: Successfully optimized routing scenarios containing up to 100 nodes while providing an accessible interface for experimentation and visualization.
engineering_decisions: Implemented the ILS-RVND metaheuristic from scratch instead of relying on optimization libraries to better control algorithm behavior.
Separated optimization logic from visualization to improve maintainability.
Designed modular neighborhood operators for easier experimentation and future extensions.
Used Streamlit to rapidly transform an optimization algorithm into an interactive application.
challenges: Implementing multiple neighborhood search operators correctly.
Balancing solution quality with computational efficiency.
Visualizing optimization results in an intuitive way for end users.
lessons_learned: Well-designed heuristics can produce near-optimal solutions within practical computation times.
Algorithm implementation quality is as important as algorithm selection.
Visualization greatly improves users' understanding of optimization results.

metrics.performance: Supports optimization scenarios up to 100 nodes

archive.title: MALIKA: AI Adaptive Learning Platform
archive.tagline: An AI-powered adaptive learning platform that personalizes mathematics education through intelligent tutoring and assessment.
archive.summary: Built a full-stack educational platform integrating Generative AI to deliver personalized learning experiences, adaptive quizzes, and AI-assisted tutoring within a culturally contextualized mathematics curriculum.
archive.problem: Traditional digital learning platforms provide identical learning experiences for every student, making it difficult to accommodate different learning styles and provide immediate personalized assistance.
archive.solution: Developed a Laravel-based web application that integrates Gemini AI to generate personalized tutoring sessions, adaptive quizzes, and automated evaluations while incorporating Malang cultural elements into mathematics learning.
archive.impact: Successfully implemented in a classroom of 25 students, achieving an N-Gain score of 0.92 (High Category) with a 97% media validity score, demonstrating significant improvement in students' understanding of permutation and combination concepts.
engineering_decisions: Integrated Generative AI as an educational assistant instead of replacing the teacher's role.
Designed modular AI services to separate business logic from AI interactions.
Implemented AI-generated quizzes with automatic evaluation to provide immediate learning feedback.
Built the application as a full-stack system to ensure seamless interaction between educational content, AI services, and user management.
challenges: Designing AI interactions that remain educational rather than simply providing answers.
Balancing personalization with curriculum consistency.
Integrating AI features into a traditional web application architecture.
lessons_learned: AI creates the greatest educational value when supporting, rather than replacing, the learning process.
Good educational systems require thoughtful UX design as much as strong AI capabilities.
Personalization significantly improves student engagement when implemented carefully.

archive.title: Aerial House Detection for Location Intelligence
archive.tagline: A computer vision system for detecting residential buildings from aerial imagery to support business location analysis.
archive.summary: Contributing to the development of a computer vision pipeline that detects and counts residential buildings from aerial imagery, enabling location intelligence for business feasibility analysis.
archive.problem: Estimating residential density traditionally requires manual surveys, making location analysis expensive, slow, and difficult to scale.
archive.solution: Prepared and curated large-scale aerial image datasets while contributing to the object detection pipeline using YOLOv8. The system estimates residential density within configurable geographic areas to support business decision-making.
archive.impact: Successfully annotated more than 5,000 aerial images and contributed to a model achieving approximately 91% detection accuracy.
engineering_decisions: Built a custom dataset by combining publicly available aerial imagery with manually collected data.
Established annotation guidelines to maintain dataset consistency across varying image resolutions and perspectives.
Focused on improving data quality to increase model robustness against different roof colors, shapes, and scales.
challenges: High visual variation between residential buildings.
Differences in image resolution and acquisition altitude.
Occlusion and dense urban environments.
lessons_learned: High-quality datasets are often more valuable than increasingly complex model architectures.
Consistent annotation standards significantly improve model reliability.
Geospatial computer vision introduces challenges beyond conventional object detection.

archive.title: SieR-Te School Information System
archive.tagline: A scalable backend system powering a modern school information management platform.
archive.summary: Developed the backend architecture for a school information system from scratch, providing secure REST APIs to support student management, attendance, scheduling, academic journals, and classroom administration.
archive.problem: Schools require centralized digital systems to manage academic activities efficiently while supporting multiple user roles such as administrators, teachers, students, and parents.
archive.solution: Designed the relational database schema and implemented a modular backend using Go Fiber, GORM, and PostgreSQL with JWT authentication and role-based access control.
archive.impact: Delivered 50+ RESTful API endpoints covering core academic modules while establishing a scalable backend architecture for future mobile application integration.
engineering_decisions: Designed the relational database schema before API implementation to maintain data consistency.
Implemented JWT authentication and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) to support multiple user roles securely.
Applied pagination and modular API design to improve maintainability and scalability.
Structured the project following clean REST API principles for easier frontend integration.
challenges: Designing a relational database with multiple interconnected academic entities.
Managing authorization rules across different user roles.
Maintaining API consistency while multiple modules were developed simultaneously.
lessons_learned: Database design has a significant impact on long-term backend maintainability.
Well-designed API contracts simplify frontend collaboration.
Planning system architecture early reduces technical debt during implementation.

archive.title: Kampus Sehat
archive.tagline: A university healthcare platform that simplifies medical service and event reservations.
archive.summary: Led the end-to-end development of a healthcare web platform for a university research project, providing online appointment booking, health event registration, and administrative management.
archive.problem: Students often rely on manual procedures to access campus healthcare services and health-related events, resulting in inefficient administration and limited accessibility.
archive.solution: Developed a full-stack web application using Laravel, MySQL, and Tailwind CSS, implementing appointment scheduling, event booking, authentication, and an administrative dashboard.
archive.impact: Delivered a centralized digital platform that streamlines healthcare service management and supports future expansion for student self-service features.
engineering_decisions: Designed a modular Laravel architecture to simplify future feature expansion.
Built reusable CRUD modules to accelerate development.
Designed a normalized relational database to support appointment scheduling and event management.
Worked directly with the client throughout requirement gathering and development.
challenges: Translating research requirements into a usable software product.
Designing scheduling logic for appointments and events.
Balancing development speed with maintainable code.
lessons_learned: Requirement clarification is as important as implementation.
Simple user experience often requires careful backend design.
Client communication significantly influences project success.

archive.title: BISINDO Real-Time Detection
archive.tagline: A real-time BISINDO alphabet detection system powered by YOLOv8.
archive.summary: Developed a real-time BISINDO alphabet detection system using the YOLOv8 object detection architecture to recognize hand gestures directly from live camera input.
archive.problem: Most BISINDO recognition research focuses on offline evaluation rather than real-time interaction, limiting its practicality for everyday communication.
archive.solution: Built a real-time detection pipeline using YOLOv8 capable of recognizing BISINDO alphabet gestures directly from webcam input with low-latency inference.
archive.impact: Demonstrated the feasibility of deploying object detection models for interactive sign language recognition and became the foundation for further research on BISINDO generalization.
engineering_decisions: Selected YOLOv8 to achieve real-time inference performance.
Built a custom training dataset tailored for BISINDO alphabet detection.
Focused on balancing inference speed with recognition accuracy.
challenges: Maintaining stable detection under varying lighting conditions.
Handling gesture similarity between multiple BISINDO characters.
Collecting sufficiently diverse training samples.
lessons_learned: Real-time deployment introduces challenges beyond offline evaluation.
Dataset diversity significantly influences detection robustness.
Inference speed is equally important as model accuracy for interactive applications.

archive.title: Mushroom Classification
archive.tagline: A machine learning application for classifying edible and poisonous mushrooms.
archive.summary: Developed a supervised machine learning application that classifies mushrooms as edible or poisonous through exploratory data analysis, feature preprocessing, model training, and web deployment.
archive.problem: Correctly distinguishing edible and poisonous mushrooms is challenging for non-experts and requires reliable classification based on observable characteristics.
archive.solution: Performed exploratory data analysis, feature preprocessing, and implemented a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier before deploying the prediction model through a Flask-based web application.
archive.impact: Demonstrated a complete end-to-end machine learning workflow from data exploration and model development to deployment as an interactive web application.
engineering_decisions: Conducted exploratory data analysis before model selection to better understand feature distributions.
Selected KNN as a strong baseline due to the categorical nature of the dataset.
Separated prediction logic from the user interface using Flask.
challenges: Handling categorical feature encoding.
Selecting appropriate hyperparameters for KNN.
Designing a simple and intuitive prediction interface.
lessons_learned: Good data preprocessing significantly influences model performance.
Model interpretability is valuable for educational machine learning projects.
Deployment is an essential part of the machine learning lifecycle.

archive.title: Bank Transaction Fraud Analysis
archive.tagline: Exploring banking transaction patterns to uncover potential fraudulent behavior using data analysis and clustering.
archive.summary: Performed exploratory data analysis on banking transaction data to understand customer transaction behavior and identify hidden patterns using unsupervised machine learning techniques.
archive.problem: Fraudulent financial transactions are often difficult to identify through manual inspection due to the large volume and complexity of transaction data.
archive.solution: Conducted comprehensive exploratory data analysis, feature visualization, and applied K-Means clustering to group transaction patterns and identify potential anomalies that may indicate fraudulent behavior.
archive.impact: Demonstrated how data exploration combined with unsupervised learning can reveal behavioral patterns that support fraud investigation and decision-making.
engineering_decisions: Performed exploratory analysis before modeling to better understand feature distributions and anomalies.
Applied K-Means clustering as an initial unsupervised approach for behavioral segmentation.
Focused on visual interpretation to communicate findings effectively.
challenges: Understanding high-dimensional financial transaction data.
Selecting meaningful features for clustering.
Interpreting unsupervised clustering results without labeled fraud data.
lessons_learned: Exploratory Data Analysis is often the most important stage of a data science project.
Clustering provides behavioral insights rather than definitive fraud predictions.
Data visualization significantly improves analytical decision-making.

archive.title: Singosari Fitness Management System
archive.tagline: A web-based fitness center management system designed to streamline membership and operational management.
archive.summary: Developed the backend of a fitness center management system, covering database design, business logic implementation, and CRUD functionalities to support daily operational activities.
archive.problem: Fitness centers often rely on manual administration, making membership management and operational processes inefficient.
archive.solution: Designed the relational database, implemented backend logic, and developed management modules supporting core operational workflows using PHP and MySQL.
archive.impact: Delivered a centralized management system that digitizes administrative workflows and simplifies day-to-day operations.
engineering_decisions: Started development from requirement analysis and database design before implementation.
Applied relational database normalization to maintain data consistency.
Separated business logic from presentation for easier maintenance.
challenges: Translating business requirements into database structures.
Maintaining consistency across interconnected CRUD modules.
Designing flexible data relationships for future feature expansion.
lessons_learned: Strong database design simplifies future development.
Understanding business processes is essential before writing code.
Maintainable CRUD systems depend on clean architecture rather than complex features.

archive.title: Kacang Pedes Singo E-Commerce
archive.tagline: A web-based e-commerce platform developed to digitize product sales for a local snack business.
archive.summary: Developed an e-commerce website for a local food business, providing product catalog management, customer transactions, and administrative features to support online sales.
archive.problem: Small businesses often lack an online platform to efficiently promote products and manage digital sales.
archive.solution: Built a complete e-commerce website using PHP and MySQL, implementing product management, shopping workflows, and administrative functionalities.
archive.impact: Demonstrated fundamental web application development skills through the implementation of a complete CRUD-based e-commerce system.
engineering_decisions: Designed a relational database to support products, customers, and transactions.
Separated administrative features from customer-facing pages.
Focused on building a maintainable CRUD-based architecture.
challenges: Designing transaction workflows.
Managing relationships between products and orders.
Building a complete web application from scratch.
lessons_learned: Database design is fundamental to e-commerce applications.
Simple and maintainable architecture is preferable for small business systems.
Understanding business requirements is as important as implementation.
